About

Disaster Watch is a live weather, storm-risk, and earthquake monitoring dashboard, built as a student project.

Aosaf Ahbab Rayeed
BUET · EEE
Prionta Pandit
BUET · EEE
Fahim Ibn Malik
BUET · EEE

Weather and storm risk come from live forecast data (Open-Meteo), so genuine hours-ahead lead time is possible for wind and rain events. Earthquake data comes from the USGS global feed, refreshed automatically — this reflects quakes as they're detected and located (typically within 1–2 minutes), not a dedicated seismometer-network countdown like Japan's JMA or Mexico's SASMEX. Where the epicenter is far enough away, the estimate below can still arrive shortly before slower shaking reaches you; near an epicenter it will not. Always follow official local authorities during a real emergency.
